facet_description: Failure emergence refers to how failure arises inherently from data structures and system rules, focusing on the structural constraints that characterize failure and the conditions under which it is triggered.

Failure experience refers to how systems represent failure in perceptible and interpretable forms, enabling players to perceive constraints and understand failure outcomes by presenting pre-failure constraints and encoding failure.

Failure response refers to how players react to failure and take subsequent actions to adjust, understand, and reassess it, including action adjustment, exploratory learning, and interpretive reframing.
